text.title: GitHub Actions — Deployment Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules including a `skip-this-methodology` rule. Each pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Production environments MUST use `environment:` with required approvers + wait-timer + branch protection. Direct pushes to prod-deploy workflows from feature branches are rejected.
rule.rationale: Without protection, any contributor can ship to prod; named approvers + branch gates restore the change-control surface.

rule.statement: Deployment logic MUST live in a reusable workflow (`uses: org/repo/.github/workflows/deploy.yml@vX`) tagged with semver. Inline duplicated deploy steps across repos are rejected.
rule.rationale: Duplicated deploy YAML drifts; centralised reusable workflows give one fix point.

rule.statement: Production deploys MUST use canary (gradual traffic shift) or blue-green (swap-and-rollback) — never an in-place all-at-once update of >25% of fleet.
rule.rationale: In-place updates have no rollback fast path; canary/blue-green make rollback a button-press.

rule.statement: Each prod deploy MUST attach a metric-based rollback gate (error rate, P95 latency, success ratio) with explicit thresholds + automatic rollback action on breach.
rule.rationale: Manual rollback is too slow during incidents; metric-bound automatic rollback caps blast radius.

rule.statement: Releases MUST be automated: tag, changelog generation from PRs, GitHub Release with artefacts. Manual tagging is rejected.
rule.rationale: Manual releases drift in format; automation gives reproducible, audit-friendly artefacts.

rule.statement: Skip when single-env without progression, OR manual deploys cannot be automated, OR no health metrics for rollback gate.
rule.rationale: Without progression/automation/metrics the methodology degrades to ceremony; fix the gaps first.
